title: Conceptualisations of critical thinking in academic writing at a master’s level
author: Andrew Gordon Drybrough
abstract: Critical thinking is often considered to involve a set of skills required by graduates of higher educational institutions in the UK. However, the conceptualisation of critical thinking by university tutors varies across disciplines and is not always particularly clear. In a UK higher educational context where proportionally increasing numbers of postgraduate students are international, often from …
relevance: 这篇论文探讨高等教育中批判性思维的概念化及其在不同学科、不同文化背景学生间的理解差异，与用户的研究有一定间接关联。首先，它涉及学术评估标准（如导师对"批判性思维"的界定）如何因学科和文化而异，这与方向一中评估中的认识论不公正（epistemic injustice）相呼应——当国际学生的批判性思维传统不被认可或被边缘化时，可能构成一种知识权力的不对等。其次，论文关注不同教育传统背景的学生与导师之间的理解落差，这

author: Andrew Gordon Drybrough
abstract: Critical thinking is often considered to involve a set of skills required by graduates of higher educational institutions in the UK.
